Phase-66.NAME: Minimal Resend Email Template Polish
Phase-66.objective: Simplify verification and password-reset Resend email templates while preserving one-time link safety, CTA clarity, text fallback, and provider/deploy boundaries.
Phase-66.non-goals: Changing Resend provider wiring, EMAIL_FROM, deploy/install scripts, DNS/BIMI behavior, frontend public/user/admin pages, auth token semantics, password policy, billing, VPN, or MTProto runtime.

requirements.requirement-1: Verification email must use a larger logo and remove visible KrotPN Matrix Access, protected-registration eyebrow, long ownership/preparation intro, remote-image hint, forwarding warning, and generic account-security footer copy.
requirements.requirement-2: Password reset email must use a larger logo and remove visible KrotPN Matrix Access, recovery eyebrow, long one-time-route intro, remote-image hint, forwarding warning if present, and generic account-security footer copy.
requirements.requirement-3: The green top strip color #5cf2c8 must be removed from the card edge and the card should keep one uniform dark surface color.
requirements.requirement-4: The outer email background currently using #04090d must become transparent or absent from the outer wrapper while the card color remains dark.
requirements.requirement-5: CTA buttons, fallback action links, localized subjects, token redaction, plain text fallback, and image-blocking usability must remain intact.

steps.step-1: Audit current backend/app/email/templates.py verification/reset shell, removed-copy list, logo sizing, top-strip styling, outer background, text fallback, and footer copy.
steps.step-2: Update the shared email template contract and renderer plan so the minimal shell keeps logo, CTA button, fallback link, and optional short safety copy only where required.
steps.step-3: Design backend email tests that assert removed strings are absent from both verification and reset HTML/text outputs while CTA and fallback URLs remain present.
steps.step-4: Design a Phase-66 smoke script that checks larger logo sizing, no #5cf2c8 top strip, no #04090d outer wrapper background, removed copy, token redaction, and protected-surface isolation.
steps.step-5: Preserve M-040 provider payload shape, M-062 one-time verification/reset semantics, M-069 public email-logo asset boundary, and M-080 visible-logo/BIMI separation.
steps.step-6: Run backend email tests, Phase-64 regression smoke, Phase-51 brand smoke, Phase-66 smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected deploy/provider/runtime guard during execution.
steps.step-7: Record execution evidence in M-079/V-M-079 and update indexes after implementation.

Phase-66.verification-design: V-M-079 defines Phase-66 gates for negative-copy assertions, logo-size assertion, removed top-strip/background assertions, CTA/fallback preservation, text fallback cleanliness, token redaction, and protected deploy/provider/runtime isolation.

verification-levels.module-local: backend/tests/test_email_delivery.py plus scripts/phase66-minimal-email-template-smoke.mjs must prove verification/reset template copy, style, action-link, fallback, and redaction behavior.
verification-levels.wave-level: scripts/phase64-premium-email-template-smoke.mjs and scripts/phase51-brand-assets-smoke.mjs must preserve Phase-64 premium email and Phase-51 brand asset baselines.
verification-levels.phase-level: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected deploy/provider/runtime guard must pass before Phase-66 implementation can be accepted.

Phase-66.acceptance: Verification and password-reset emails become visually cleaner and shorter while remaining transactional, token-safe, image-blocking tolerant, and Resend-compatible.

risk-assessment.risk-1: Over-deleting text can make image-blocked emails unclear; fallback link and concise purpose text must remain.
risk-assessment.risk-2: Template changes must not leak one-time verify/reset URLs in logs, tests, smoke output, or docs evidence.
risk-assessment.risk-3: Email clients are inconsistent; transparent outer background must degrade without hiding the dark card or CTA.
